success_surface: Conditional cell detection with styling preservation and cross-sheet data transposition
effective_behaviors: Detect and handle Excel color formats robustly (ARGB vs RGB, theme-based colors)
Filter out empty/blank values during data collection to avoid meaningless entries
Clear target cells before writing to prevent residual data conflicts
Preserve formatting (fill, border, style) when transferring data between locations
Calculate target ranges dynamically based on actual data count
Provide staged verification logs throughout execution
evidence: Agent checked ARGB format ('FFFF00' in bg_color) rather than assuming simple RGB strings
Excluded None, '', ' ', '\n', '\r' from yellow_cells collection list
Cleared master_sheet row 1 before re-inserting transposed values to ensure clean slate
Applied PatternFill(yellow_fill) and Border(thin_border) to transferred cells
Used len(yellow_cells) for max_col_needed calculation instead of hardcoded range
Printed intermediate outputs: 'Found X yellow cells', 'Transposed Y values'
Accessed cell.fill.fgColor.rgb properly instead of direct attribute comparison

memory_items.title: Excel Color Format Detection
memory_items.lesson: Always check the full ARGB format structure when detecting cell background colors, accounting for theme-based and indexed color types.
memory_items.generalization: When filtering cells by color, access cell.fill.fgColor.rgb and search for partial matches (e.g., 'FFFF00' for yellow) while handling None/null cases.

memory_items.title: Data Sanitization During Extraction
memory_items.lesson: Filter empty values during iteration to avoid transferring meaningless or placeholder entries.
memory_items.generalization: Before appending collected cells to a result list, validate against common emptiness indicators: None, empty string, whitespace characters, and newline symbols.

memory_items.title: Destination Cell Preparation
memory_items.lesson: Clear the target range completely before writing new transposed or copied data.
memory_items.generalization: When overwriting a cell range, first iterate through the affected area and set value=None and reset styles to avoid conflicts with stale content.

memory_items.title: Style Preservation in Transfers
memory_items.lesson: When moving formatted cells, apply the same fill, border, and style attributes to the destination.
memory_items.generalization: After setting cell.value, explicitly assign cell.fill, cell.border, and any other relevant style properties to match the source formatting.

memory_items.title: Dynamic Range Computation
memory_items.lesson: Calculate target dimensions from the actual data count rather than hardcoding ranges.
memory_items.generalization: Use len(collected_data) to determine output column/row spans, ensuring the operation adapts to variable input sizes.

memory_items.title: Staged Execution Logging
memory_items.lesson: Log key milestones: detection counts, processing status, and completion confirmation.
memory_items.generalization: Print summary information after data collection, during transfer operations, and upon file save to enable debugging and verification.

edits.content:
## 6. Trace-Derived Lessons

### Color & Style Detection
- **ARGB Color Handling**: When filtering by cell color, always access `cell.fill.fgColor.rgb` and check for partial hex matches (e.g., `'FFFF00'`) rather than exact equality. Account for theme-based colors that may return `None` for RGB.
- **Empty Value Filtering**: During cell collection iterations, exclude entries where value is `None`, `''`, whitespace only, or newline characters to avoid meaningless transfers.

### Data Transposition Best Practices
- **Clear Before Write**: Reset all cells in the target range (set value to `None`, clear fills/borders) before inserting new transposed data to eliminate stale content conflicts.
- **Style Replication**: After assigning cell values, explicitly copy `cell.fill`, `cell.border`, and other formatting properties from source to destination cells.
- **Dynamic Sizing**: Calculate output dimensions from the actual collected dataset length rather than using hardcoded ranges.
- **Execution Logging**: Emit progress summaries after data discovery, during transformation, and after file save to enable monitoring and troubleshooting.
